text: To stop saturation attacks on this board by spammers it was necessary to put a condition on new members that the first post requires moderator approval to be visible to the group.  So if you are just coming here, it may take a little time for your first post to appear because of the approval process.
title: NEWCOMERS BE AWARE OF THIS

text: I will try and check frequently for new posts and approval all that are from genuine new members, not spammers.  We regret this is necessary however those who were here during that last round of malicious and overwhelming spamming understand why this was required.
title: NEWCOMERS BE AWARE OF THIS

text: Thanks for your patience.
title: NEWCOMERS BE AWARE OF THIS

text: A little reminder of how this Board operates is in order after yet another thread opened by an unpopular poster became the target of personal attack and abuse from the beginning of a thread they started.
title: Reminder:  YOU CAN PERSONALLY IGNORE POSTERS YOU FIND TO BE OBNOXIOUS, TEDIOUS, WEIRD OR WHATEVER

text: First and most important, YOU can personally censor someone here you don't like simply by adding them to your ignore list.
title: Reminder:  YOU CAN PERSONALLY IGNORE POSTERS YOU FIND TO BE OBNOXIOUS, TEDIOUS, WEIRD OR WHATEVER

text: To do this, go to your personal settings, click on "Ignored Users" and add that person's Board name exactly aa shown on the left hand side of their post, to your list.  Be sure to type it exactly as it appears.
title: Reminder:  YOU CAN PERSONALLY IGNORE POSTERS YOU FIND TO BE OBNOXIOUS, TEDIOUS, WEIRD OR WHATEVER

text: You will no longer see their thread or post content.  THIS IS FAR MORE EFFECTIVE in shutting down obnoxious but permitted content then going to the thread and starting a personal pissing match.
title: Reminder:  YOU CAN PERSONALLY IGNORE POSTERS YOU FIND TO BE OBNOXIOUS, TEDIOUS, WEIRD OR WHATEVER

text: Ignored user threads fall off the first page pretty quick.
title: Reminder:  YOU CAN PERSONALLY IGNORE POSTERS YOU FIND TO BE OBNOXIOUS, TEDIOUS, WEIRD OR WHATEVER

text: If you are having difficulty doing this, PM myself or Pete and I'm sure we can help you with it.
title: Reminder:  YOU CAN PERSONALLY IGNORE POSTERS YOU FIND TO BE OBNOXIOUS, TEDIOUS, WEIRD OR WHATEVER

text: Basically if one choses to be a tedious pain in the butt here (in the opinion of other or others) that is their choice and so long as one's posting does not get involved in religion, politics, libel of local people or businesses or this Board, or abusive personal attack it is OK.
title: Reminder:  YOU CAN PERSONALLY IGNORE POSTERS YOU FIND TO BE OBNOXIOUS, TEDIOUS, WEIRD OR WHATEVER

text: If you think a post or thread is doing this, please PM myself or Pete immediately with details of where the problem is and we will address it ASAP..  Neither the Mods or the Admn reads everything posted here so it is up to you all to help out by alerting us to problems.
title: Reminder:  YOU CAN PERSONALLY IGNORE POSTERS YOU FIND TO BE OBNOXIOUS, TEDIOUS, WEIRD OR WHATEVER

text: In summary, YOU have the right and the means to censor another poster from your page, YOU SINGLY OR IN PLURAL DO NOT have the right to block the speech of others which you don't personally like but that does not violate the rules of this group.
title: Reminder:  YOU CAN PERSONALLY IGNORE POSTERS YOU FIND TO BE OBNOXIOUS, TEDIOUS, WEIRD OR WHATEVER

text: Thank you for your cooperation.
title: Reminder:  YOU CAN PERSONALLY IGNORE POSTERS YOU FIND TO BE OBNOXIOUS, TEDIOUS, WEIRD OR WHATEVER

text: Due to overwhelming demand and the need to educate people on traffic laws, their rights and responsibilities as well as fines and amounts, I have updated my Jalisco Traffic Guide so it is current as of October 19, 2018. Please feel free to read it and download it and let me know if there is anything that should be added / deleted / corrected, etc.
title: New 2018 Jalisco Driving Guide

text: Had a fairly good sized tree succumb to the wet and wind and fall over two days ago. Emailed Chapala Tree Service, they responded immediately and came quickly to look at, emailed me in a couple hours with a very reasonable quote to completely remove and came out the same day, yesterday, and did a very good job including thorough cleanup.
title: Can't Say Enough Good About Chapala Tree Service

text: When Roberto came to collect this morning they had cut a section of the tree out that had two orchids on it without being asked. I just assumed the orchids wouldn't surve the process but they made sure they did.
title: Can't Say Enough Good About Chapala Tree Service

text: I just can't say enough good about Chapala Tree Service.  Great atttitude, quality of work, reliability, fair and honest pricing, they have it all.  Thumbs up for this great local company.
title: Can't Say Enough Good About Chapala Tree Service
Can't Say Enough Good About Chapala Tree Service

text: Matteo Volpi is a project leader for Senderos y Caminos de México A.C., which was founded in Ajijic in 2014 to help people find their way along Mexico’s vast network of rural trails, and also to rescue, rehabilitate and preserve these ancient footpaths, some of which were in use long before the arrival of the Conquistadores.
title: Reviving Mexico’s rural trails,  Chapala, Ajijic, Jocotepec, and Ixlahuácan y mas

text: Matteo Volpi shows the official Senderos trail marker—a bright orange triangle—to rancher Felipe Callejas, who lives deep inside the forest between Mascota and Puerto Vallarta.
title: Reviving Mexico’s rural trails,  Chapala, Ajijic, Jocotepec, and Ixlahuácan y mas

text: In 2021, Senderos published a 144-page Trail Guide for de los Membrillos. It comes in both a Spanish and an English version and contains 100 color photos as well as 26 maps featuring contour lines, estimates of hiking time and levels of difficulty.
title: Reviving Mexico’s rural trails,  Chapala, Ajijic, Jocotepec, and Ixlahuácan y mas

text: One of the organization’s newest projects, Matteo Volpi told me, is what Senderos will call the Chapala Trail Network.
title: Reviving Mexico’s rural trails,  Chapala, Ajijic, Jocotepec, and Ixlahuácan y mas

text: “It’s a project we’ve been working on for ten years. There are, of course, a great many trails above the lake shore, but what we want to do is to create a mini network inside the bigger network.
title: Reviving Mexico’s rural trails,  Chapala, Ajijic, Jocotepec, and Ixlahuácan y mas

text: “We want to get at least six trails into really good shape and then have five different axes, heading to Chupinaya, El Caracol, Ixtlalhuacan, La Cañada and to Las Trojes.  So it will look like a spider, and people can say, ‘I’ll start in El Caracol and finish in Ajijic’ or ‘I can start in Ajijic and finish up in La Cañada,’ or you can create your own trail.
title: Reviving Mexico’s rural trails,  Chapala, Ajijic, Jocotepec, and Ixlahuácan y mas

text: “To renovate and signpost each leg of the spider, we’ll be working very closely with the local community.”
title: Reviving Mexico’s rural trails,  Chapala, Ajijic, Jocotepec, and Ixlahuácan y mas

text: Perhaps Senderos de Mexico’s most ambitious project is a 70-kilometer-long trail that starts in Mascota at an altitude of 1,300 meters and takes trekkers down to Playa Grande Ecopark on the Pacific Coast, near Puerto Vallarta.
title: Reviving Mexico’s rural trails,  Chapala, Ajijic, Jocotepec, and Ixlahuácan y mas

text: Currently, the electromobility project is in an operational testing phase on the main corridor; technicians are verifying travel times and operational accuracy.
title: Pablo Lemus announces the opening of Line 5 on June 4th

text: The system will operate with a fleet of 41 Volvo Luminus BZR buses, which are 100% electric.So far, eight of these vehicles are already in the city for preliminary testing. Each unit has a capacity to transport 80 passengers. They have air conditioning, free wifi, for a first-world transportation system that will connect to the Guadalajara airport.
title: Pablo Lemus announces the opening of Line 5 on June 4th

text: To reinforce the system's operation, new articulated 7800 Electric units, zero-emission options for urban mobility, will also arrive in the city in the coming days.. According to authorities, these buses are equipped with high-performance dual electric motors and batteries up to 720 kWh. They offer extended range, energy efficiency, and a fast-charging system of up to 250 kW.
title: Pablo Lemus announces the opening of Line 5 on June 4th

text: Designed for large cities, they have a capacity for 130 passengers.
title: Pablo Lemus announces the opening of Line 5 on June 4th

text: The entrance to the city presented a significant challenge, with an investment of approximately 11 billion pesos in state funds to completely transform the urban landscape at the airport entrance.
title: Pablo Lemus announces the opening of Line 5 on June 4th

text: They have widened this access road to 12 lanes, added hydraulic concrete, a bike path, street lights, and trees, which will give the city a new look, they are also working to improve the visual appearance of the airport.
title: Pablo Lemus announces the opening of Line 5 on June 4th

text: Visit this event organized by local beekeepers and get a lot of honey-derived products and foods. For three days you can discover 100% local honey and enjoy cultural events about bees and hive products, as part of World Bee Day , which is celebrated every May 20th. It will take place from May 23rd to 25th , from 10:00 AM to 8:00 PM , in San Nicolás de Bari Park , in front of the sanctuary.
title: Guadalajara Honey Fair

text:
Admission is free. Organized by the Local Livestock Association of Beekeepers of Guadalajara , this fair seeks to promote local consumption and highlight the importance of bees for the balance of the ecosystem. Among the products you will find are:
	
	Honey from different flowers
	Pollen and propolis
	Mead
	Handmade sweets
	Natural soaps, creams and shampoos
title: Guadalajara Honey Fair

text: Are you receiving messages about traffic tickets? It's a scam!
title: Text messages about traffic fines

text: The Mexico City Secretariat of Citizen Security (SSC) warned of a digital fraud in which cybercriminal groups use websites that impersonate official government platforms to steal users' personal and financial data.
title: Text messages about traffic fines

text: A form of fraud is receiving messages about supposed fines with malicious links.
title: Text messages about traffic fines

text: The  authorities warned that information entered on fake websites can be used for identity theft or financial fraud, urging citizens not to enter personal data , passwords, or banking information on unofficial or dubious websites.
title: Text messages about traffic fines

text: The Cyber Police of the SSC recommended that users verify that domains have the .gob.mx extension and the https security certificate as an initial filter of authenticity.
title: Text messages about traffic fines

text: Source: Aristegui Noticias
title: Text messages about traffic fines
